methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $232M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Lunar Energy, a Series D climate and energy company founded by Kunal Girotra, has raised $232M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private and one public comparable company operating in the same sector and stage range, including Redwood Materials, KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $232M total raised as a proxy valuation and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yielding $348M. The Peer-Median method benchmarks against four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.5B, though the peer range is wide—spanning $700M at the low end (Amogy) to $6.0B at the high end (Redwood Materials at Series D). This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within frontier energy.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $296M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $924M. The Stage-Progression method anchors the lower bound; the Peer-Median method dominates the upper range. Key uncertainties include Lunar Energy's specific technology readiness relative to peers and the degree to which Redwood Materials' outlier $6B valuation reflects unique market position versus sector-wide inflection in capital availability for decarbonization solutions.
